summary: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0 and also exposed a Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview build on Arena, marking the clearest product artifact in the current set. Anthropic changed Claude Code economics by requiring extra payment for third-party harness usage such as OpenClaw starting April 4, while AgentScope 1.0 and VibeVoice surfaced as notable new research artifacts but without the benchmark evidence needed to rank them as clear breakthroughs.

top_topics.HEADLINE: ByteDance Ships Seed 2.0 and Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: ByteDance Seed officially announced Seed 2.0 through its own channels, making this an on-record model launch rather than a leak or benchmark rumor.
The company also released Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, indicating ByteDance is putting an early variant into public comparative evaluation workflows.
The same announcement stream included ByteDance Seed's 2027 foundation-model campus recruiting push, a signal that the company is pairing product launch activity with continued talent investment.

top_topics.title: AgentScope 1.0: A Developer-Centric Framework for Building Agentic Applications
top_topics.summary: Driven by rapid advancements of Large Language Models (LLMs), agents are empowered to combine intrinsic knowledge with dynamic tool use, greatly enhancing their capacity to address real-world tasks. In line with such an evolution, AgentScope introduces major improvements in a new version (1.0), towards comprehensively supporting flexible and efficient tool-based agent-environment interactions for building agentic applications. Specifically, we abstract foundational components essential for agentic applications and provide unified interfaces and extensible modules, enabling developers to eas...

top_topics.HEADLINE: AgentScope 1.0 Launches as a Framework for Agentic Application Development
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: AgentScope 1.0 is positioned as a developer-centric framework for building agentic applications, targeting workflows where LLMs combine intrinsic reasoning with external tool use.
The release surfaced as a distinct versioned artifact—1.0—suggesting the project is moving from experimentation toward a more stable platform posture.
Based on the available evidence, the announcement emphasizes architecture and developer experience rather than publishing hard benchmark wins against competing agent frameworks.

top_topics.title: VibeVoice Technical Report
top_topics.summary: This report presents VibeVoice, a novel model designed to synthesize long-form speech with multiple speakers by employing next-token diffusion, which is a unified method for modeling continuous data by autoregressively generating latent vectors via diffusion. To enable this, we introduce a novel continuous speech tokenizer that, when compared to the popular Encodec model, improves data compression by 80 times while maintaining comparable performance. The tokenizer effectively preserves audio fidelity while significantly boosting computational efficiency for processing long sequences. Thus,...

items.title: VibeVoice Technical Report

To enable this, we introduce a novel continuous speech tokenizer that, when compared to the popular Encodec model, improves data compression by 80 times while maintaining comparable performance. The tokenizer effectively preserves audio fidelity while significantly boosting computational efficiency for processing long sequences. Thus,...

metadata.ai_summary: VibeVoice synthesizes long-form multi-speaker speech using next-token diffusion and a highly efficient continuous speech tokenizer, achieving superior performance and fidelity.

To enable this, we introduce a novel continuous speech tokenizer that, when compared to the popular Encodec model, improves data compression by 80 times while maintaining comparable performance. The tokenizer effectively preserves audio fidelity while significantly boosting computational efficiency for processing long sequences. Thus,...
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: If validated, a model that improves long-form multi-speaker synthesis could matter for media generation, dubbing, and conversational audio products where coherence over extended outputs is a bottleneck.
top_topics.HEADLINE: VibeVoice Technical Report Introduces Long-Form Multi-Speaker Speech Generation
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: VibeVoice proposes long-form speech synthesis with multiple speakers using next-token diffusion, a design intended to unify autoregressive generation with diffusion-based latent modeling.
The report specifically targets continuous long-horizon audio generation, an area where speaker consistency and temporal stability are often weak points in existing systems.
The currently available description is technical and architectural; it does not yet provide enough benchmark detail in the evidence here to establish a clear state-of-the-art claim.

top_topics.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
top_topics.summary: Anthropic changed its Claude Code subscription policy so that using OpenClaw and similar third-party tools will require additional payment. The change takes effect on April 4 and increases the cost of that workflow for subscribers.

items.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
items.summary: Using OpenClaw with Claude AI is about to get a lot more expensive, thanks to Anthropic's new policy changes. Beginning April 4th at 3PM ET, users will "no longer be able to use your Claude subscription limits for third-party harnesses including OpenClaw," according to an email sent to users on Friday evening. Instead, if users […]

items.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
items.summary: It’s about to become more expensive for Claude Code subscribers to use Anthropic’s coding assistant with OpenClaw and other third-party tools.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: Anthropic changed its Claude Code subscription policy so that using OpenClaw and similar third-party tools will require additional payment. The change takes effect on April 4 and increases the cost of that workflow for subscribers.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: This policy shift affects the cost structure of developer tooling built around Claude, which matters for budgeting, partner strategy, and the viability of third-party agent wrappers.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Anthropic Changes Claude Code Pricing for OpenClaw and Third-Party Harnesses
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: Anthropic told users that beginning April 4 at 3 PM ET, Claude subscription limits can no longer be used for third-party harnesses including OpenClaw.
The change means Claude Code subscribers who rely on OpenClaw or similar tools will need to pay extra, directly raising usage costs for some developer workflows.
The move was independently reported by both The Verge and TechCrunch, suggesting this is a material platform-policy change rather than an isolated customer-support issue.

topics.title: research-llm-apis 2026-04-04
topics.summary: Simon Willison published a new release of the research-llm-apis project dated 2026-04-04. The post announces an update to the software and links to the corresponding GitHub release.

items.title: research-llm-apis 2026-04-04
items.summary:
Release: research-llm-apis 2026-04-04

I'm working on a major change to my LLM Python library and CLI tool. LLM provides an abstraction layer over hundreds of different LLMs from dozens of different vendors thanks to its plugin system, and some of those vendors have grown new features over the past year which LLM's abstraction layer can't handle, such as server-side tool execution.

To help design that new abstraction layer I had Claude Code read through the Python client libraries for Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini and Mistral and use those to help craft curl commands to access the raw JSON for both streaming a...

topics.title: Show HN: sllm – Split a GPU node with other developers, unlimited tokens
topics.summary: A Show HN post introduced sllm, a service for sharing access to a GPU node among multiple developers. The post frames it as a way to use large models such as DeepSeek V3 with lower individual cost by reserving a slot in a shared cohort.

items.title: Show HN: sllm – Split a GPU node with other developers, unlimited tokens
items.summary: Running DeepSeek V3 (685B) requires 8×H100 GPUs which is about $14k/month. Most developers only need 15-25 tok/s. sllm lets you join a cohort of developers sharing a dedicated node. You reserve a spot with your card, and nobody is charged until the cohort fills. Prices start at $5/mo for smaller models. The LLMs are completely private (we don't log any traffic). The API is OpenAI-compatible (we run vLLM), so you j...

topics.SHORT_COMMENT: A Show HN post introduced sllm, a service for sharing access to a GPU node among multiple developers. The post frames it as a way to use large models such as DeepSeek V3 with lower individual cost by reserving a slot in a shared cohort.
topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: This tooling surfaced across 1 independent source(s).
topics.HEADLINE: Show HN: sllm – Split a GPU node with other developers, unlimited tokens

topics.title: PaddleOCR-VL: Boosting Multilingual Document Parsing via a 0.9B Ultra-Compact Vision-Language Model
topics.summary: In this report, we propose PaddleOCR-VL, a SOTA and resource-efficient model tailored for document parsing. Its core component is PaddleOCR-VL-0.9B, a compact yet powerful vision-language model (VLM) that integrates a NaViT-style dynamic resolution visual encoder with the ERNIE-4.5-0.3B language model to enable accurate element recognition. This innovative model efficiently supports 109 languages and excels in recognizing complex elements (e.g., text, tables, formulas, and charts), while maintaining minimal resource consumption. Through comprehensive evaluations on widely used public benchm...

topics.title: Generative World Renderer
topics.summary: Scaling generative inverse and forward rendering to real-world scenarios is bottlenecked by the limited realism and temporal coherence of existing synthetic datasets. To bridge this persistent domain gap, we introduce a large-scale, dynamic dataset curated from visually complex AAA games. Using a novel dual-screen stitched capture method, we extracted 4M continuous frames (720p/30 FPS) of synchronized RGB and five G-buffer channels across diverse scenes, visual effects, and environments, including adverse weather and motion-blur variants. This dataset uniquely advances bidirectional renderi...

topics.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
topics.summary: A trending paper on Hugging Face describes PagedAttention, a method for more efficient memory management in LLM serving. The paper focuses on improving throughput by handling KV-cache memory more effectively during batched inference.

items.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
items.summary: High throughput serving of large language models (LLMs) requires batching sufficiently many requests at a time. However, existing systems struggle because the key-value cache (KV cache) memory for each request is huge and grows and shrinks dynamically. When managed inefficiently, this memory can be significantly wasted by fragmentation and redundant duplication, limiting the batch size. To address this problem, we propose PagedAttention, an attention algorithm inspired by the classical virtual memory and paging techniques in operating systems. On top of it, we build vLLM, an LLM serving sys...

metadata.ai_summary: PagedAttention algorithm and vLLM system enhance the throughput of large language models by efficiently managing memory and reducing waste in the key-value cache.
metadata.spotlight_comment: High throughput serving of large language models (LLMs) requires batching sufficiently many requests at a time. topics.title: A decoder-only foundation model for time-series forecasting
topics.summary: Motivated by recent advances in large language models for Natural Language Processing (NLP), we design a time-series foundation model for forecasting whose out-of-the-box zero-shot performance on a variety of public datasets comes close to the accuracy of state-of-the-art supervised forecasting models for each individual dataset. Our model is based on pretraining a patched-decoder style attention model on a large time-series corpus, and can work well across different forecasting history lengths, prediction lengths and temporal granularities.

topics.title: Very Large-Scale Multi-Agent Simulation in AgentScope
topics.summary: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have opened new avenues for applying multi-agent systems in very large-scale simulations. However, there remain several challenges when conducting multi-agent simulations with existing platforms, such as limited scalability and low efficiency, unsatisfied agent diversity, and effort-intensive management processes. To address these challenges, we develop several new features and components for AgentScope, a user-friendly multi-agent platform, enhancing its convenience and flexibility for supporting very large-scale multi-agent simulations. Spec...

paper_spotlight.label: Daily Hot Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that feel broadly important to the day and belong in the hotspot paper feed.

items.title: VibeVoice Technical Report
items.summary: This report presents VibeVoice, a novel model designed to synthesize long-form speech with multiple speakers by employing next-token diffusion, which is a unified method for modeling continuous data by autoregressively generating latent vectors via diffusion. To enable this, we introduce a novel continuous speech tokenizer that, when compared to the popular Encodec model, improves data compression by 80 times while maintaining comparable performance. The tokenizer effectively preserves audio fidelity while significantly boosting computational efficiency for processing long sequences. Thus,...

This innovative model efficiently supports 109 languages and excels in recognizing complex elements (e.g., text, tables, formulas, and charts), while maintaining minimal resource consumption. items.title: AgentScope 1.0: A Developer-Centric Framework for Building Agentic Applications
items.summary: Driven by rapid advancements of Large Language Models (LLMs), agents are empowered to combine intrinsic knowledge with dynamic tool use, greatly enhancing their capacity to address real-world tasks. In line with such an evolution, AgentScope introduces major improvements in a new version (1.0), towards comprehensively supporting flexible and efficient tool-based agent-environment interactions for building agentic applications. Specifically, we abstract foundational components essential for agentic applications and provide unified interfaces and extensible modules, enabling developers to eas...

x_buzz.title: Show HN: sllm – Split a GPU node with other developers, unlimited tokens
x_buzz.summary: Running DeepSeek V3 (685B) requires 8×H100 GPUs which is about $14k/month. Most developers only need 15-25 tok/s. sllm lets you join a cohort of developers sharing a dedicated node. You reserve a spot with your card, and nobody is charged until the cohort fills. Prices start at $5/mo for smaller models. The LLMs are completely private (we don't log any traffic). The API is OpenAI-compatible (we run vLLM), so you j...
